channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Is Pokémon Champions on Nintendo Switch & Mobile? All Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
It’s a big year for Pokémon fans with the 30th anniversary of the franchise, and another new title is on the horizon in the form of Pokémon Champions.

Fresh off the back of Pokopia‘s widely successful release, alongside the re-release of Fire Red and Leaf Green, Pokémon is pushing hard in 2026, and Pokémon Champions is set to result in a sweep of changes in the competitive scene.

If you’re interested in the release of Pokémon Champions and want to know what platforms the game is available on, Insider Gaming has all the details.

Is Pokémon Champions on Nintendo Switch?

Yes, Pokémon Champions is available on both generations of the Nintendo Switch console, so those yet to upgrade to a Nintendo Switch 2 will not miss out.

While Nintendo is fully shifting into the Switch 2 era, putting the likes of Pokopia as a next-gen exclusive title, support for the original consoles has not ended completely, and a select few releases will be available on both generations.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon Champions Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

Pokémon Champions is among those available on both eras of the console, and cross-generation play means you can face off against someone on a Nintendo Switch 2, regardless of which console you own, and vice versa.

For those on Nintendo Switch 2, an update will be available for Pokémon Champions, rather than a standalone version, providing enhanced visual performance. The update will be free.

Is Pokémon Champions on Mobile?

Pokémon Champions is confirmed to be coming to mobile devices, but the launch will occur later than the Nintendo Switch release on April 8.

A precise release window for Pokémon Champions on mobile has not been announced, but it has been stated that the game will be released on mobile devices at some point in 2026.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pokémon Champions Will Have Balance Patches

This will likely occur in the second half of the year, after the Pokémon World Championships in San Francisco in August, which will give plenty of time to iron out any issues that may occur from the mobile port.

Pokémon Champions is set to become the title that hosts the VGC series of competitive Pokémon, and the availability on mobile devices opens the door to a wider range of players.

Are you excited for the release of Pokémon Champions? Share your thoughts on the game in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon Champions, read about the first VGC events that will use the game, and check out the Battle Pass pricing in Japan.

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Mini Crossword Hints Today—Answers For Wednesday, April 1
item.content:encoded:
The NYT Mini Crossword is a popular, quick-to-complete word game to test yourself daily, but some of the puzzles can be difficult to complete, so here are some hints.

If you’ve found yourself stuck on today’s NYT Mini, Insider Gaming has some helpful hints and all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on Wednesday, April 1, 2026.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of the NYT Mini Crossword. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How to Play NYT Mini Crossword

The NYT Mini Crossword is available to play on the New York Times Games app or website, but, as of September 2025, a New York Times Game subscription is required.

The NYT Games Subscription is priced at $6.95 per month, but there are introductory offers available for other NYT subscriptions that include games access, which might be worth considering.

Without a Games subscription, access to the NYT Mini Crossword is blocked.

NYT Mini Crossword Clues (April 1)

Here are all the clues provided for the NYT Mini Crossword for April 1, 2026.

Across

1 – YYYYYYY

5 – Back half of an old cassette tape

6 – Perform lines that aren’t in the script

7 – GGGGGGG

8 – Butt, to Brits

Down

1 – Opposite of narrower

2 – Keeps the engine on while parked

3 – CCCCCCC

4 – ___ and flow (tidal opposites)

5 – Long, epic tale

Additional Clues For NYT Mini (April 1)

Here are some additional clues to help you solve the NYT Mini Crossword on April 1, 2026.

Across

1 – Old man trait

5 – Two sides, second side name

6 – Like improv

7 – Express shock

8 – “He’s such an ____”

Down

1 – “Vehicles ___ than these restrictions should not pass under the bridge”

2 – Similar to lazy, slothful

3 – To take, often done by police

4 – Movement of tide out to see

5 – The Infinity ___, first major part of the Marvel Cinematic Universe

All NYT Mini Crossword Answers (April 1)

Here are all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on April 1, 2026.

Across

1 – WISE

5 – SIDEB

6 – ADLIB

7 – GEEZ

8 – ARSE

Down

1 – WIDER

2 – IDLES

3 – SEIZE

4 – EBB

5 – SAGA

When is The Next Game?

The NYT Mini Crossword refreshes daily at 10 PM ET on weekdays and Saturdays, and at 6 PM ET on Sundays.

As such, the next NYT Mini Crossword will be available at 7 PM PT/9 PM CT/10 PM ET/3 AM GMT/2 PM AET.

What is The NYT Mini Crossword?

The NYT Mini Crossword was introduced in 2014 as a shorter and quicker-to-complete game to accompany the main New York Times Crossword, which was initially released in 1942.

Until September 2025, the NYT Mini Crossword was free, but it has now been consolidated into a monthly NYT Games Subscription alongside several other popular games.

Did you struggle with the NYT Mini Crossword today? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Xbox Announces ‘Game Pass Flex’, Replacing Loading Screens with Ads
item.content:encoded:
Xbox has just unveiled Game Pass Flex, a free service that leans on the extensive library and digital streaming power of the Xbox ecosystem to bring a selection of the platform’s top titles to players on a budget. The only caveat is that Game Pass Flex takes traditional loading screens and replaces them with full-screen ads.

This means that, as you play one of the games under the Game Pass Flex banner, you’ll be regularly subjected to unskippable, unavoidable ads. But hey, the service is totally free, so what more do you want?

Game Pass Flex is the Answer to Expensive Subscriptions

If you’ve been wanting to dip your toe into the Game Pass ecosystem without parting ways with your hard-earned cash, then the Game Pass Flex service might be for you, given how it’s totally a real thing…

Today, Xbox announced the instant arrival of this free tier, which is supported by an innovative ad-based system.

When you play a game via Game Pass Flex, you’ll be presented with full-screen ads every time there’s a loading screen.

The team at Xbox also confirmed that users can play for longer to earn ‘Ad Tokens’ that will allow them to skip certain ads earlier, presenting a nice incentive to keep gamers grinding for hours on end.

In a statement, Xbox’s VP of Strategic Honesty, Nott Reely, said:

At Xbox, we’re committed to delivering what we call Transparent Value Alignment. With our new Game Pass Flex tier, we’re giving players more choice than ever, allowing them to enjoy wonderful gaming experiences fused with contextually enriching advertisements.

Some might call them interruptions, but we prefer to think of them as immersive micro-moments that boost engagement and retention.

Speaking exclusively to Insider Gaming on the news, Bethesda Game Studios’ Hodd Toward explained:

This is nothing but great news for gamers worldwide. With Game Pass Flex, we can leverage our patented loading screens, which you’ll see across every game we have ever made, to promote all kinds of products and services.

This is why we still have such extensive loading screens across our gaming estate. We could have seamless transitions between interiors and exteriors, but now we can use that pause to inject an ad into your experience, keeping you guessing about what might come next.

Game Pass Flex launches today, April 1st, but it’s available exclusively for users who have never subscribed to Game Pass before. That means you can’t cancel your paid subscription to accept the ad-supported tier.

Do you think you’ll take advantage of Game Pass Flex? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you fell for this blatant joke of a post.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that GTA 6 might be getting a ‘crime-free’ mode at launch

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up? Fortnite April 1 Downtime, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite players should be well accustomed to regular downtime by now, but when the servers are not available, it can raise alarm bells for those who aren’t aware of what is going on.

A fresh patch in Fortnite is being deployed on April 1, bringing version 40.10 onto the scene, and with it comes fresh content to enjoy, including a new game mode.

If you’re impatiently waiting for the Fortnite servers to return, here are all the details on when they will be available.

When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up?

Fortnite servers are down for several hours for the April 1 update, although the exact time the servers will return is never usually confirmed by Epic Games.

Downtime on April 1, which marks the start of Fortnite OG Season 8, begins at 2 AM ET. While it’s not officially confirmed how long the servers will be down for, previous experience gives us an expected time frame.

Usually, downtime on mid-season updates is not as long as the seasonal transitions, like the move to Chapter 7, Season 2, and instead, downtime is around three to four hours on average.

Using that metric, the Fortnite servers should be back up somewhere around 4 AM to 5 AM ET on April 1, though this is subject to change, especially if any issues arise while the patch is deployed.

As usual, the best place to keep an eye on the Fortnite server status is social media.

What’s In The Fortnite April 1 Update?

The biggest tweak coming in the April 1 Fortnite update is Season 8 of Fortnite OG, which rolls it back to Chapter 1, Season 8 from 2019.

Build-up for the switch has been taking place all week, with earthquakes on the Fortnite OG island ahead of the volcano’s emergence, which will bring POIs and overall map refreshes to the scene.

Elsewhere, the patch will also get things set for the return of the Avengers limited-time mode, which launches Friday. Just like it was in 2019, teams will be split into Avengers and the Chitauri, with a head-to-head last team standing battle. However, there are updates to the loot pool.

In addition to those content drops, the Super Styles in the Battle Pass are expected to be revealed, while there could also be leaks ahead of Save The World’s imminent move to a free-to-play model.

Are you excited for Season 8 of Fortnite OG? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, read about the return of the Avengers LTM, and check out all Week 2 quests in Chapter 7, Season 2.

MOBILE APP
